answer:
**Morning purification** (encompassing waking, evacuation/*śauca*, oral hygiene/*dantadhāvana*, ritual sipping/*ācamana*, and bathing) is outlined as a foundational daily obligation for Brahmins in both texts.[^1][^2]

In *Manusmṛti* 4.92–93, a Brahmin must **awaken in the Brahma-muhūrta** (roughly 96 minutes before sunrise), contemplate *dharma* alongside worldly concerns and Vedic truths, then **rise, perform "necessary duties"** (implicitly evacuation and initial cleansing), **purify himself**, and stand devotedly for the morning twilight (*prātah sandhyā*), reciting sacred texts at length—emulating the sages who thereby gained longevity and luster.[^3]

*Viṣṇu Smṛti* elaborates this sequence across chapters 60–64, aligning with the Brahma hour rising but expanding into granular rules:

- **Ch. 60**: **Rise at the "hour of Brahma"** specifically for evacuation, facing south at night/north by day/twilights; avoid ~20 prohibited sites (e.g., ploughed earth, shadows, anthills, cow-pens, water); purify (*śauca*) anus/penis/feet/hands with exact lumps of earth (3 on anus, 10 on one hand post-penis, etc.), scaling up (2x–4x) for ascetics.[^4]
- **Ch. 61**: Clean teeth with a twig (*dantakaṣṭha*) from 14 approved trees (e.g., Vaṭa, Bilva—bitter/pungent/astringent only), ~12 finger-breadths long, chewed silently facing north/east; explicitly "having risen in the morning." Avoid 15+ forbidden woods (e.g., Palāśa, Śleṣmātaka) or defective twigs (sour, hollow, etc.).[^5]
- **Ch. 62**: Perform *ācamana* (sipping) seated stably facing east/north, using pure cool water via hand's "sacred parts" (*tīrthas*: e.g., Brāhma at thumb-root, sipped 3x; wipe lips 2x; touch head orifices/heart).[^6]
- **Ch. 64**: Bathe post-reddish dawn (not naked/sick/twilight/night, except eclipses); in running/Ganges water ideally; consecrate with specific mantras (*āpo hi ṣṭhā*, etc.); mutter *Aghamarṣaṇa* (3x) or alternatives; perform *tarpaṇa* to gods/ancestors in wet clothes using Daiva/Pitrya *tīrthas*; then don fresh garments.[^7]

**Viṣṇu adds exhaustive procedural minutiae Manu skips**: e.g., precise earth-lump counts/sites/directions for post-evacuation *śauca*; allowable/forbidden tooth-twig woods and dimensions; exact hand-*tīrthas* for *ācamana*; bathing mantras (*āpo hi*, *Puruṣasūkta*, etc.), *tarpaṇa* sequencing, and wet-clothes rule—rendering the rite more ritually precise and prophylactic against impurity.[^8]

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 4, [translation/](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/).

[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, Chs. 60–64, [Ch. 60 translation/](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/60/translation/).

[^3]: *Manusmṛti* 4.92–93, [para. p-c7d753](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-c7d753) (contextual range from read; awakening/purification/twilight).

[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 60.1–26, [para. p-d04dc6](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/60/translation/#p-d04dc6) (Brahma hour/evacuation/*śauca*).

[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 61.1–17, [para. p-d23a81](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/61/translation/#p-d23a81).

[^6]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 62.1–9, [para. p-3149d2](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/62/translation/#p-3149d2).

[^7]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 64.1–42, [para. p-411b47](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/64/translation/#p-411b47) (mantras/*tarpaṇa*).

[^8]: Cf. *Manusmṛti* 4.45–56 (evacuation/bath prohibitions, but no counts/mantras); 4.92–93 (terse "purify").
